Exited founder + venture builder, helping startups and corporate teams go from zero to product-market fit—and beyond to exit. I thrive on turning ambitious ideas into real products and ventures with speed, clarity, and execution.

I’m the Founder of Build Momentum, a hands-on venture studio that makes it easier for founders, enterprise teams, and investors to spot high-impact opportunities, launch new ventures, and scale with confidence. We don’t just advise—we build alongside you. Through Fractional CPO leadership, 90-day innovation sprints, end-to-end product launches, and a network of seasoned venture operators, we reduce risk, save time, and help you unlock growth faster than going it alone.

I’ve led ventures across AI, enterprise SaaS, mobility, eCommerce, real estate, and mental health—taking companies from scratch to revenue, scaling into new countries and markets, leading cross-functional teams, and building corporate partnerships to unlock new growth. I operate globally, bilingual in English and Spanish, with experience building across borders.

Fun facts: I don’t shy from a challenge—once biking across the U.S. from Boston to San Francisco in 50 days. I’ve lived and worked in five countries (U.S., South Korea, the Philippines, Colombia, and Mexico). I also coach first-time LGBTQ founders. And when I’m not building ventures, I’m training for my next triathlon.

byos0GFY86SHMmBCIh0Z.name: Arizona Commerce Authority
byos0GFY86SHMmBCIh0Z.bio:
The Arizona Commerce Authority (ACA) is the state's leading economic development organization with a streamlined mission to grow and strengthen Arizona’s economy. The ACA uses a three-pronged approach to advance the overall economy: recruit, grow, create – recruit out-of-state companies to expand their operations in Arizona; work with existing companies to grow their business in Arizona and beyond; and partner with entrepreneurs and companies large and small to create new jobs and businesses in targeted industries.

The ACA is overseen by a prestigious public-private sector board of directors bringing together industry, academia and government. Chaired by Arizona Governor Katie Hobbs, the board is comprised of the state’s most prominent private-sector business leaders, university and community college presidents, and elected officials.

The ACA’s executive management team drives the organization’s day-to-day operations, ensuring it maintains focus on the recruitment of quality companies and jobs to the state of Arizona. This team brings together the best and brightest in economic development, management and communications.

Shkvarun Ivan
CEO & co-founder Social Links Inc and the author of DarksideAI initiative 


I have 15+ years of experience in automation of various sectors of the economy, experience in leading positions in international IT companies, and was the head of the financial and public sector at the SAP. A mathematician by first degree and I have an MBA degree in entrepreneurship.

I first came across the idea of ​​using Open Data 20 years ago and since then it has been my passion. In 2015, we launched Social Links company with my partners, for 3 years it was a hobby and search for PMF, since 2018 we have been actively growing and have become recognised world leaders in OSINT. We have over 350+ clients in over 80+ countries.

We are testing how our products can empower other areas of the economy now, such as cybersecurity, finance, sales & marketing. There are successful pilots and projects already.

We closed the first investment round A at the end of 2022, we were bootstrap company before. We plan to raise Series B in the first quarter of 2025.

I will be glad to meet investors and partners who will help develop OSINT and Open Data market  together.

sd4kKBUFd2V0qGCDaQKt.bio:
Founded in 2006, Columbia Venture Community (CVC) is a private, global network of more than 7,500 Columbia University alumni, students, and staff who are interested in all aspects of entrepreneurship and innovation. The group welcomes members from any Columbia University school, sector, or industry function (e.g., entrepreneur, investor, designer, lawyer, etc.).

Our mission is to foster entrepreneurship among Columbia University alumni, students, and staff by facilitating communication, providing access to needed resources, and creating a vibrant and supportive community. To that end, we gather for an array of virtual and in-person events including incubation nights, demo nights, speaker panels, private dinners, and happy hours. We have hosted 500+ events since our founding.

CVC is endorsed by Columbia University and is classified as a shared interest group (SIG) under the Columbia Alumni Association (CAA).

A decision-grade identity risk score
20+ explainable signals validating identity coherence and authenticity

Heka is an API-first, zero-installation solution designed by former intelligence analysts from elite units, and backed by Barclays Bank.

NYTM is NY Tech Alliance's signature event that has been a staple for the NYC tech ecosystem since 2004. It's the world’s largest meetup group of its kind with members who have a common interest in learning from and helping one another. At each event, entrepreneurs, founders, CEOs, and their teams demo cutting edge and transformative tech, answer questions from attendees and network with all in a welcoming, fun-filled space. Since its creation, over 1,000 companies have participated and have been a driving force of innovation and job creation in NYC and the greater NY area.

This event is a perfect place to start if you are new to tech, to New York City, or to the New York tech ecosystem. It’s all about meeting people and having a "front row seat" to learn of the latest innovations that address market opportunities.

UeslnxCHmaYmNSerxkYgkGjkmOT2.name: Women Who Code
UeslnxCHmaYmNSerxkYgkGjkmOT2.bio:
Women Who Code is a global nonprofit organization dedicated to advancing women in technology in technology Careers. We believe that innovation thrives when diverse perspectives shape the future — and we are committed to closing the gender gap in tech by equipping women with the skills, community, and opportunities they need to lead.

Our work focuses on accelerating women technologists from experienced contributors and managers into decision-makers, executives, founders, and board leaders — ensuring women are not only participating in tech, but shaping its direction.

Founded in 2011 by engineers, Women Who Code grew into a vibrant global community of nearly half a million technologists across 145 countries. The organization delivered more than 20,000 community-led technical trainings, awarded $3.5 million in scholarships, and contributed over one million volunteer hours in service to the tech ecosystem. Through its global network, members accessed more than 14,000 career opportunities while developing the leadership capabilities needed to advance and thrive.

In April 2024, Women Who Code closed due to funding challenges. However, its impact and mission continue. TechFutures is proud to welcome Women Who Code into its family of brands, carrying forward the legacy of its pioneering founders. Together, we are committed to building on this foundation and continuing to support and elevate women in technology worldwide.

nNsAdQYFJhRrK3YCbyRx3SMvBKB2.bio:
Lorine is the Founder/Managing Partner of 125 Ventures, an early-stage VC fund investing in sports (with a particular focus on women's sports, media, and entertainment at the intersection of tech). Lorine Pendleton is a seasoned VC, advisor and attorney with extensive experience in the legal, technology and entertainment industries. She was named by Mergers & Acquisitions as the 2021 Top 25 Most Influential Women in Mid-Market M&A, by Worth as one of the “16 Financial Powerhouses,” Marie Claire as one of the “Most 50 Connected Women in America – Star Dealmaker,” Brown Brothers Harriman’s Women & Wealth as “21 Women to Watch in 2021, Business Insider as one of the “22 Female Angel Investors Every Startup Founder Should Know,” Crunchbase as “39 Black Women Investors Inspiring a New Generation of Investors,” Medium as one of the “200 Women of Color to Follow on Twitter,” and Black Enterprise as one of the “20 Angel Investors You Should Know.” 

Lorine launched our investing career a decade ago. Initially as an angel investor and now VC. She launched and ran Portfolia’s Rising America Fund I & II, which invests in companies with founders of color, women, LGBTQI+ or companies with products or services addressing those markets. Her fund has made 25+ investments to date including notable investments such as Maven Health ($1.3B valued women’s health company), Madison Reed (revolutionizing hair color), Canela Media (Latinx streaming service),  and MoCaFi (digital bank for the under- and unbanked). She is an angel investor in Oura Ring (sleep and fitness tracker), Rhode Island Football Club (USL League pro men's soccer team) and it's $140M Centreville Bank Staidum. She is a former Board Member of the Angel Capital Association and a former Entrepreneur-in-Residence at Brown University’s Nelson Center for Entrepreneurship. She was recently appointed to serve on the President’s Advisory Council for Entrepreneurship at Brown University. She is a Special Committee Member of the University of Buffalo Foundation Endowment Investment Committee and a Member of the Investment Committee for the New York State’s Venture Fund.

iotofiFdwZNkPBzRH4LyTPM4qcE2.name: Jessica Santiago
iotofiFdwZNkPBzRH4LyTPM4qcE2.bio:
Jessica Santiago is a leading digital art curator and the founder of ArtRepublic Global, a curatorial platform of contemporary digital and immersive art that is purpose-built for wellness. She has made it her life’s work to empower artists and translate this new medium to brands, corporations, and institutions through an act of modern patronage. Recently, Santiago and her AR team have cultivated impactful projects with Samsung, United Nations, Deepak Chopra, Uhealth and SuperRare to name a few. Together with these esteemed co-collaborators, she has produced prolific exhibits and events that have played an important role in advancing the digital art medium. 

As an early supporter of digital art, Santiago knew that she had to create demand and open the minds of many to an immersive, dynamic artistic experience. Her innovative spirit speaks to creatives and businesses alike, who seek to help shape contemporary culture. She’s built community amongst her network of artists and developed an audience by consistently delivering relevant, meaningful bodies of work. 

Santiago is dedicated to helping artists thrive in this new realm by giving them access to all facets of today’s groundbreaking tech ecosystem. She mindfully selects partners that align with ArtRepublic’s ethos to build authentic engagement. Her curation is deeply intuitive. Her exhibitions and activations are meant to inspire thought-provoking environments. Santiago has established relationships with renowned artists, marketplaces, and art-centric brands from all over the world, with the intention to ignite economic growth, bridge cultural divides and foster global wellbeing.
